title: dubautia × kohalae
intro: Dubautia × kohalae is a rare, sterile hybrid plant species, resulting from the cross between two different species of Dubautia, a genus of flowering plants in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and flowers, which have been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Dubautia × kohalae include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin issues.
